{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 神澤神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|伊豆村|Q11380579|伊豆}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E|display=inline,title}}

{{coord2|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}
}}
'''神澤神社''' ('''神沢神社''')（かんざわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}西方近隣に鎮座している<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''氐良命神社'''（てらのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 手間天神（てまてんじん）/氐良命（てらのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の4番目の子「てい」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神

== Japanese content ==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}
}}
'''神澤神社''' ('''神沢神社''')（かんざわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}西方近隣に鎮座している<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''氐良命神社'''（てらのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 手間天神（てまてんじん）/氐良命（てらのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の4番目の子「てい」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''神澤神社''' ('''神沢神社''')（かんざわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}西方近隣に鎮座している<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''氐良命神社'''（てらのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 手間天神（てまてんじん）/氐良命（てらのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の4番目の子「てい」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

'''神澤神社''' ('''神沢神社''')（かんざわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}西方近隣に鎮座している<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''氐良命神社'''（てらのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 手間天神（てまてんじん）/氐良命（てらのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の4番目の子「てい」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}

'''神澤神社''' ('''神沢神社''')（かんざわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}西方近隣に鎮座している<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''氐良命神社'''（てらのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 手間天神（てまてんじん）/氐良命（てらのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の4番目の子「てい」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}
}}
'''神澤神社''' ('''神沢神社''')（かんざわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}（{{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

三宅島北西部の伊豆地区の山中、{{jalink|御祭神社|Q131925937}}西方近隣に鎮座している<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''氐良命神社'''（てらのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 手間天神（てまてんじん）/氐良命（てらのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の4番目の子「てい」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神

{{DEFAULTSORT:かんさわしんしや}}

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Kamisawa Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}}{{jalink|Izu Village|Q11380579|Izu}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E|display=inline,title}}

|神事   =

{{coord2|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}
}}
'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Kamisawa Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}
}}
'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{jalink|Omiyajinja|Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{jalink|Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}』</ref>.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}』</ref>.

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}』</ref>.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Kamisawa Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Izu Village|Q11380579|WD=Q11380579|lt=Izu|1=Izu}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

{{神社

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Kamisawa Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Izu Village|Q11380579|WD=Q11380579|lt=Izu|1=Izu}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E|display=inline,title}}

{{coord2|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|6|40|N|139|29|57.8|E}}}}
}}
'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|WD=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.

{{nihongo|'''Kamisawa Shrine'''|神澤神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kamisawa Shrine''' ('''Kanzawa Shrine''') (かんざわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake, Tokyo|Q1154797|qid=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|qid=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}})<ref name="yao">[https://yaokami.jp/1131734/ 神澤神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140121-01.html 神沢神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>.

It is located in the mountains of the Izu area in the northwestern part of Miyake-jima, near the west side of {{ill|lt=Omiyajinja|Q131925937|qid=Q131925937|1=Gosai-jinjna}}<ref name="miyake">[https://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/kankousasshi_0503.pdf 三宅島 (p.68, p.84)] - 東京都/三宅島観光協会</ref>.

It is identified with '''Terano-Mikoto Shrine''' (てらのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}<ref name="yao"/><ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Tematenjin (てまてんじん) / Tera-no-Mikoto (てらのみこと) - identified as "Tei," the fourth child among the eight princes born between {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sataotamahime-no-Mikoto (enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mikasa Shrine|Q110915859|qid=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="miyake"/><ref>『{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|qid=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}』</ref>.
